label: NVDA — Q3 FY2025 Earnings Beat + Data Center Blowout
description: NVIDIA reported Q3 FY2025 revenue of $35.1B vs. consensus $33.1B (+6.1% beat). Data Center revenue of $30.8B grew 112% YoY. EPS of $0.81 beat by $0.06. Guided Q4 to $37.5B ±2%, above consensus of $37.0B.
pre_loaded_text: NVIDIA CORPORATION REPORTS FINANCIAL RESULTS FOR THE THIRD QUARTER FISCAL 2025. Santa Clara, California - November 20, 2024 - NVIDIA (NASDAQ: NVDA) today reported revenue for the third quarter ended October 27, 2024, of $35.1 billion, up 17% from the previous quarter and up 94% from a year ago. GAAP earnings per diluted share for the quarter were $0.78, up 16% from the previous quarter and up 111% from a year ago. Non-GAAP earnings per diluted share were $0.81, up 19% from the previous quarter and up 103% from a year ago. Revenue by Market Platform: Data Center revenue was a record $30.8 billion, up 17% from the previous quarter and up 112% from a year ago. Gaming revenue was $3.3 billion, up 14% from the previous quarter and up 15% from a year ago. Professional Visualization revenue was $486 million, up 7% from the previous quarter and up 17% from a year ago. Automotive revenue was $449 million, up 30% from the previous quarter and up 72% from a year ago. OEM and Other revenue was $108 million. Outlook: NVIDIA's outlook for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 is: Revenue is expected to be $37.5 billion, plus or minus 2%. GAAP and non-GAAP gross margins are expected to be 73.0% and 73.5%, respectively, plus or minus 50 basis points. CEO Jensen Huang said: 'The age of AI is in full steam, propelling a global shift to NVIDIA computing. Demand for Hopper and anticipation for Blackwell - in full production - are incredible as foundation model makers scale pretraining, post-training, and inference.'

label: AMD — Q3 2024 Guidance Cut: Gaming & Embedded Weakness
description: AMD reported Q3 2024 revenue of $6.82B (+18% YoY), but guided Q4 to $7.5B ±$300M, below prior guidance of $7.7B. Gaming segment declined 69% YoY due to semi-custom revenue normalization. MI300X AI GPU sales tracking to $5B for 2024.
pre_loaded_text: AMD REPORTS THIRD QUARTER 2024 FINANCIAL RESULTS. SANTA CLARA, Calif., Oct. 29, 2024 - AMD (NASDAQ: AMD) today announced revenue for the third quarter of 2024 of $6.82 billion, gross margin of 50%, operating income of $724 million, net income of $771 million, and diluted earnings per share of $0.47. On a non-GAAP basis, gross margin was 54%, operating income was $1.72 billion, net income was $1.50 billion, and diluted earnings per share was $0.92. Q3 2024 Highlights: Data Center segment revenue was $3.55 billion, up 122% year-over-year and up 25% sequentially, driven by strong sales of AMD Instinct GPU and EPYC CPU products. Client segment revenue was $1.88 billion, up 29% year-over-year and up 26% sequentially. Gaming segment revenue was $462 million, down 69% year-over-year and down 29% sequentially, reflecting reduced semi-custom revenue. Embedded segment revenue was $927 million, up 25% year-over-year. Q4 2024 Outlook: Revenue of approximately $7.5 billion, plus or minus $300 million. This represents approximately 22% year-over-year growth. Non-GAAP gross margin of approximately 54%. AMD CEO Dr. Lisa Su commented: 'We delivered strong revenue growth in the third quarter driven by our Data Center segment which set a new quarterly revenue record. MI300X GPU sales have ramped extremely well and remain on track to exceed $5 billion in 2024.'

label: AMZN — Q2 2024 Earnings Beat + AWS Acceleration
description: Amazon reported Q2 2024 revenue of $148.0B vs $148.6B consensus (slight miss) but operating income of $14.7B crushed the $13.0B consensus. AWS grew 19% YoY to $26.3B. Guided Q3 operating income to $11.5B-$15.0B above the $13.3B consensus.
pre_loaded_text: AMAZON.COM ANNOUNCES SECOND QUARTER RESULTS. SEATTLE - August 1, 2024 - Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN) today announced financial results for its second quarter ended June 30, 2024. Net sales increased 10% to $148.0 billion in the second quarter, compared with $134.4 billion in second quarter 2023. Operating income increased to $14.7 billion in the second quarter, compared with $7.7 billion in second quarter 2023. Net income increased to $13.5 billion in the second quarter, or $1.26 per diluted share, compared with $6.7 billion, or $0.65 per diluted share, in second quarter 2023. AWS net sales were $26.3 billion in Q2 2024, up 19% year over year, driven by customer demand for cloud computing and generative AI services. North America segment operating income was $5.1 billion for Q2 2024, an 8.1% operating margin versus 3.9% in the prior year period. Andy Jassy, Amazon's President and CEO, said: 'Q2 was a strong quarter across the board. AWS continues to reaccelerate, driven by companies both optimizing their existing workloads and committing to new long-term deals.' Q3 2024 Guidance: Net sales between $154.0 billion and $158.5 billion, representing growth of 8% to 11% year over year. Operating income between $11.5 billion and $15.0 billion.

label: META — Q3 2024 Earnings Beat (RESTRICTED TICKER — mandate kill)
description: Meta beat on revenue ($40.6B vs $40.3B est) and EPS ($6.03 vs $5.25 est) — but META sits on the fund's restricted ticker list, so the agent must kill the trade regardless of how bullish the signal is. Demonstrates the mandate gate.
pre_loaded_text: META REPORTS THIRD QUARTER 2024 RESULTS. MENLO PARK, Calif. - October 30, 2024 - Meta Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ: META) today reported financial results for the quarter ended September 30, 2024. Revenue was $40.59 billion, an increase of 19% year-over-year. Net income was $15.69 billion, an increase of 35% year-over-year. Diluted earnings per share was $6.03, compared to analyst consensus of $5.25. Family daily active people (DAP) was 3.29 billion on average for September 2024, an increase of 5% year-over-year. Ad impressions delivered across our Family of Apps increased 7% year-over-year and the average price per ad increased 11% year-over-year. Reality Labs operating loss was $4.43 billion. CEO Mark Zuckerberg said: 'We had a good quarter driven by AI progress across our apps and business. We also have strong momentum with Meta AI, Llama adoption, and AI-powered glasses.' CFO Outlook: We expect fourth quarter 2024 total revenue to be in the range of $45-48 billion. We anticipate our full-year 2024 total expenses to be in the range of $96-98 billion. We continue to expect significant capital expenditures growth in 2025 as we invest in AI infrastructure, with capital expenditures in the range of $38-40 billion for full-year 2024.

label: INTC — Q2 2024 Guidance Cut + Dividend Suspension
description: Intel guided Q3 revenue to $12.5-$13.5B vs $14.3B consensus, announced a $10B cost reduction plan with 15%+ headcount cuts, and suspended its dividend. Stock fell 26% the next day. Tests the agent's bearish-event handling against a long-only mandate.
pre_loaded_text: INTEL REPORTS SECOND QUARTER 2024 FINANCIAL RESULTS; ANNOUNCES $10 BILLION COST REDUCTION PLAN. SANTA CLARA, Calif. - August 1, 2024 - Intel Corporation (NASDAQ: INTC) today reported second quarter 2024 financial results. Second quarter revenue was $12.8 billion, down 1% year-over-year. GAAP loss per share was $(0.38); non-GAAP EPS was $0.02, below guidance. Gross margin of 35.4% declined 3.1 percentage points year-over-year, driven by the accelerated ramp of AI PC products and higher charges related to non-core businesses. Q3 2024 Business Outlook: Intel expects revenue of $12.5 billion to $13.5 billion, below analyst consensus of $14.3 billion, and non-GAAP EPS of $(0.03). Cost Reduction Plan: Intel announced a comprehensive reduction in spending, including a greater than 15% reduction in headcount, targeting $10 billion in cost savings for 2025. The company expects to complete the majority of headcount actions by the end of 2024. Dividend Suspension: Intel announced it will suspend its dividend starting in the fourth quarter of 2024, prioritizing liquidity to support investments needed to execute its strategy. CEO Pat Gelsinger said: 'Our Q2 financial performance was disappointing, even as we hit key product and process technology milestones. Second-half trends are more challenging than we previously expected.'
